claim: ImageNet at internet scale drove the deep learning revolution in computer vision more than algorithmic changes; compute is often underestimated as the key unlock compared to papers like AlexNet or Transformers
actionable: When building AI systems, prioritize access to large-scale data and compute before optimizing algorithm complexity; data and compute often matter more than architectural cleverness
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Between GTX 580 (2010, 6-day AlexNet training) and GB200 (2024), raw compute increased by ~5000x; training that took 6 days on two GPUs now takes ~5 minutes on one GPU
actionable: Account for exponential compute growth when planning multi-year research; what seems computationally infeasible today may be commodity-level in 2-3 years
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Supervised learning era (ImageNet) required explicit human labels; generative/self-supervised era unlocked training on unlabeled data, enabling much larger datasets without annotation bottleneck
actionable: Shift training focus from labeled data to unlabeled at scale; use self-supervised and generative approaches to bypass human annotation costs
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Reconstruction and generation in computer vision are converging: you can reconstruct a real scene or generate a novel one using similar techniques (NeRF, diffusion)
actionable: Apply reconstruction techniques to generation problems and vice versa; the mathematical and algorithmic similarities enable transfer between tasks
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Language models use 1D sequence representations (tokens); spatial intelligence requires 3D representations. Multimodal LLMs shoehorn images into 1D tokens, losing 3D structure critical for interaction
actionable: For tasks requiring spatial reasoning (robotics, AR/VR), design models with native 3D representations rather than flattening to 1D token sequences
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Language is a purely generated signal (no words exist in nature); 3D world has intrinsic physical laws and structure. This fundamental difference means spatial intelligence cannot simply apply LLM approaches
actionable: Build spatial models that respect physics and 3D geometry rather than borrowing pure generation paradigms from language; leverage physics constraints as inductive bias
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: NeRF (Neural Radiance Fields, 2020) enabled 3D reconstruction from 2D observations on a single GPU in hours; this algorithmic breakthrough made 3D vision research feasible in academia without massive compute
actionable: Look for algorithmic breakthroughs that decouple compute requirements from problem difficulty; these enable broader research participation and faster iteration
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Spatial intelligence requires understanding 3D/4D world structure to enable interaction and manipulation; this goes beyond perception to enabling action (robotics, AR, object manipulation)
actionable: Design systems for spatial intelligence that enable action primitives (move, manipulate, interact) not just perception; ground learning in interaction outcomes
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Virtual world generation as a new media form becomes economically viable if AI reduces creation costs below current game-development levels; this unlocks personalized, niche content generation
actionable: Invest in spatial intelligence models for world generation; identify content creation tasks where AI-driven generation can beat manual authoring cost/time
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Spatial intelligence for AR/VR requires seamless blending of virtual and physical content; 3D understanding enables replacing multiple screens with contextual overlays on physical world
actionable: For spatial computing interfaces, prioritize 3D representations that enable natural blending with physical space; move beyond 2D screen paradigms
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Robots need spatial intelligence to interface between digital compute and physical world; without 3D understanding of environment, robot learning cannot effectively translate to real-world behavior
actionable: For robotics, prioritize spatial intelligence models that understand 3D geometry, physics, and object relationships; train on diverse embodied perspectives
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Building world-class teams for deep tech requires gathering top experts across multidisciplinary domains (ML, graphics, systems, 3D vision); concentration of complementary talent is more important than individual stars
actionable: For complex technical problems, recruit teams of specialists across domains rather than hiring isolated experts; foster belief in shared northstar
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI

claim: Success for spatial intelligence models means widespread deployment solving real customer problems, not just academic benchmarks; long-term northstars may take years to reach but generate cascading new possibilities
actionable: Define success by real-world deployment and impact; recognize northstar problems that guide long-term R&D but expect unexpected applications to emerge
source_title: The Future of AI is Here — Fei-Fei Li Unveils the Next Frontier of AI
